answer:
\boxed{C}

Cuthbert Calculus in *The Adventures of Tintin* was inspired by **J. Cecil Maby**, a British mathematician and author of popular science books. Hergé (the creator of Tintin) often drew from real-life figures for his characters, and Maby’s work on mathematics and aviation aligned well with Calculus’s role. The other options (Will Morris, Jacques Piccard, Auguste Picard) were not the direct inspiration for this character.
